as products get better and better each year each promising a new set of breakthrough features there s something to be said about the journey through a product line within a product line last year the latitude 7340 was my first glimpse into the solid no fuss philosophy of dell s approach to business laptops fast forward to my 9440 review just last month and we witnessed a leap towards the highend or even the overbuilt hardware and that led us here eagerly anticipating the what awaits us with the 9450 the 7450 coming soon and now to review the 7440 before we jump into the 50 series let s anchor ourselves in the present with the 7440 series this machine is the very definition of staple good unassuming but it s proficient at at the core tasks it might not have the same flare of the 9440 that we just reviewed but it nails the essentials so well that it gets me really excited to see what its successor will have in store in the 7450 let s dive into the substance behind the latitude 7440 is understated elegance and where we just want maybe a bit more let s start with the design unlike the 9440 the latitude 7440 adopts more let s say understated charm its little brother that i reviewed last year the materials are quite relatively pedestrian being a combination of plastic and magnesium now while it s generally not going to turn head heads at a coffee shop it also won t strain your shoulders carrying it around in a backpack because it is significantly more light but what really stands out about this design over the more expensive 9440 is the fact that it still retains legacy ports including hdmi two usb type a ports a headphone jack in addition to the two usbc thunderbolt 4 ports despite those added ports the 7440 is still quite thin and i would frankly prefer this over having to carry around a dongle in order to connect peripherals a mouse and keyboard and speaking of connections it s not about usbs and hdmis it s also about connecting with you too if you re enjoying the deep dive into the world of business tech we d love to earn your subscription if you find our videos insightful join the no iso community as a free way to support the channel and stay connected for the journey through dell s evolving latitude line and much more now back to the 7440 now as for the webcam there s none of that magical automatic snapping shutter we had on the 9440 it s just a classic little shutter that a manual shutter that allows you to to cover it up i appreciate it for its st straightforwardness since it doesn t make little noises when it opens and closes the same my model relies just on windows for low facial recognition and no fingerprint sensor at least in this model it is an optional upgrade now i prefer having both personally but i got to say the facial recognition on on these devices is incredibly fast and a lot more consistent than what i ve experienced on most my surface laptops this model has the higher end display at 2560 x600 or 2k along with 400nits max brightness which is in my mind a sweet spot for business use it s not the micro oled some might yearn for or that we had in the 9440 but it s just as sharp and bright as i need out of a business machine and remember the 9440 had that glossy display while sharper and it became less usable and bright light when i was using dark backgrounds the 7440 with a matte finish instead performs much better under direct lighting in my experience although you do have to put up with unfortunate plastic bezels around the sides the display can also come in 1900 by 1200 which is 1080p stretch downwards and i think for most business laptops that should be sufficient this one does have touchscreen which i do appreciate touch screens on any of my laptops so it was nice to have as for under the hood this review model boasts an i5 13 generation paired with 16 gigs of ram and 500 gig ssd it s noticeably slower than an i7 and 9440 but it does handle just about every office task well once i started to throw more and more of it especially when i on when i was on video calls the performance started to lag down a little bit and the fan started to get a little bit louder dell offers the i7 processor as an option on this and up to 32 gigs of ram which could be a better fit if you re a power user me maybe it s a trade off with the size but it feels unfortunately the days of upgrading ram and business laptops this are gone so you re stuck with whatever you buy for your team that said at least the ssd slot is still accessible and and very easily upgradeable one major drawback from from its big brother though i noticed is this has significantly less battery life than the 9440 in my testing which seem to average somewhere in the ballpark around 5 to 6 hours of use before i needed to find an outlet this is more in line with expectations for most business laptops i ve used some a little bit better even so don t expect to get a full work day out of it unless you have a plug nearby if you re docked in then generally you ll just be okay now one thing i did prefer with this device over the 9440 is the keyboard keys are all in the right spot as you d expect and the amount of additional travel that you get with the spaced out keys and the comfort is quite rewarding now it s not as fancy looking as the non islanded keys of the 9440 but in my mind the spacing is very very comfortable and it feels a lot more my older 5400 latitude from a few years back me personally i also prefer this trackpad with a physical click over the capactive trackpad on the 9440 now generally that s driven by the fact that i have a relatively large large hands and i had a tendency to accidentally overlap the trackpad on the 9440 which was substantially larger and the result was i d accidentally misclick while resting my palm on the keyboard the 9440 the 7440 comes with dell s suite of business software including the dell optimizer software which unfortunately doesn t feel quite optimized for me my complaints about this software are the same as on other dell devices but generally the features of the dell optimizer software are pretty cool and it s some very very interesting additions that you don t have with native windows it s just i wish it was a little bit more fluid overall my experience is that while the 9440 was the most striking business laptop that i ve used in a long time and offered some very compelling reasons to want a business laptop the 7440 is a very consistent dependable device that perfectly satisfies my needs for a business laptop i love the fact that it s so lightweight especially with a full 14 in screen it might sound a bit bit silly but in my mind the world would be a better place if all companies use this as their primary devices but that s not unfortunately the way that things work in fact the 7440 in my mind is is the consistent standard that most business laptops should try to reach now while the battery life and the performance for my model wasn t quite outstanding i thought that 7440 felt just great and fluid and nice to use and it was in my mind a lot less stuffy than the 9440 dell s 50 series are just around the corner and i m very pumped to see what ai and other features dell has in store now if you re also excited and do you want to hear what we have to say then i d love to hear what you d me to focus on and any questions that you have for the 50 series and as we get our hands on them we will be sure to keep you updated thank you for watching ino iso i hope you this review of the dell latitude 74 40 if you did be sure to get subscribed and i ll catch you in the next one see you

3 combo card soldered into the motherboard not upgradable by the user so just keep that in mind but wi fi 6e is working great and so is the bluetooth on this unit now when it comes to the optional mobile broadband 5g there is an option here and great news because the antenna leads are already included and configured here all you need to do is just get the modem that s an option a lot of the business users would definitely want to have especially when you get worked on on the road and want to have an always on secure connection now there are two options when it comes to display full hd option 1920x1200 i don t have that one but i do have the 14 inch qhd plus option with touch layer on it it s a 2560 by 1600 resolution it s also a comfort view plus display it s an ips display and it s pretty great you re looking at deep blacks good white point good contrast you re looking at good coverage of the color gamut and it is color accurate so if you want to do some content creation in lightroom photoshop even some light video editing and maybe 1080p certainly can get the job done with this display now it s also a touch layer as i mentioned earlier that worked well when it comes to pinch to zoom navigating the os with your finger worked well now it s also a matte display so you don t have to deal with any unnecessary glare or reflections that s a big bonus especially for those who want to get work done and not be distracted by any unnecessary glare and when you want to watch a movie on your downtime then netflix amazon youtube has been very good with this panel no complaints on that front now one thing to keep in mind this is a 60 hertz refresh rate no option for higher refresh rate not too unusual any business focused ultra portable the name of the game here would be longevity and battery life which we ll get into in a moment it s really good and you don t want to be bogged down by lower battery life due to a higher refresh rate which will eat more power so it makes a lot of sense here and they also moved to a 16 to 10 aspect ratio one of the things when i looked at the 7330 last year with it 16 to 9 a rather dated aspect ratio in 2022 well this one is a 16 to 10 aspect ratio more in line with all the modern laptops here good to see that move a very welcome indeed this is the camera on the dell latitude 7440 ultralight here for 2023 it s a 5 megapixel camera it s an ir camera that means you can log in with face recognition with windows hello what do you think about the video quality which here you re seeing 1440p video what do you think about the audio quality of the mics is it doing a good job of suppressing the background noise and so forth let me know in the comment section below there s also the studio effects where you have the auto framing you also have the background blur effect and all that it s all here so if you re going to do any zoom calls video conferencing it all works well again i want to know what you think let me know in the comment section below okay let s talk about the performance and this has the core i5 1345u that of course is 10 cores eight efficient cores two performance cores intel 13 generation it is a 15 watt cpu now as you can see from these benchmark results good for everyday tasks microsoft office email web browsing it all worked well good single core score as you can see from the cinebench r23 single core score of 1707 very good for those everyday tasks and of course when you look at the multi core score very impressive for a core i5 processor here getting 97

2 gen 1 and that is great to see of course and then finally rounding out the ports is a kensington lock slot that is all the ports you get on this now notably missing there s no micro sd card reader that you got last year you don t get it this year they ve done away with that so a little bit of a downer there but overall pretty decent port selection especially for an ultra portable now to get inside the laptop it is really easy there are eight phillips head captive screws all you need to do is loosen them pry off the bottom plate with a guitar pick or pry tool and it s that easy or you re in there s nothing else to do there so once you re inside you ll notice a single fan for cooling you ll notice the 57 watt hour battery battery life by the way is pretty good we re going to get into it later on in this review now my review unit has a 2242 ssd so that is the only ssd slot in here it is m
